reviews.review_msg: Simply the best. So accommodating. Blessss him

Trevor is a rare instructor who can make you passionate about any material. The course description can seem a bit intimidating at first, especially if your background is mainly Ling but if you keep up w/ lectures, problem sets and office hours, you'll be okay. He's also very responsive over email and happy to provide extra problems if you're stuck!

Trevor was actually my TA for this course, but he does an exceptional job at reviewing lectures! He answers questions clearly, and even if you don't quite understand a concept, he takes the time to help as long as you ask. I definitely recommend going to office hours. I would love to take another class with him, whether he's a TA or the prof!!!!!

He was my TA for the class. Easy to reach, even outside of class. Made class light and informative. Answered any and all questions even helped me with other courses. Check out his Youtube channel, "trevtutor", also very helpful for linguistics people!

There are no better professors than Trevor. The pinnacle of a good prof. Very clear lectures and assignments that were perfectly relevant to the material. Always accessible to ask Qs and gives great answers.
Amazing Prof!

The grading on the papers was a tough but it was an interesting course that taught a lot about syntax. Trevor responds quickly to all of my emails and met on Zoom whenever I asked. I recommend this course if you took LING 322 already!

Trevor was a great professor. What stood out the most was his ability to teach the content in a simple way that helps you to have a good understanding. To get the most out of the course you just have to watch or go to the lectures and pay attention, the rest should come fairly easy. His clear explanations helped me learn a lot from this class.
This course was so fun! The lectures were great and the project was a lot of fun. I am not a linguistics major but I wish found this class earlier so I could take linguistics courses in my degree.
This man is fantastic. He gets back to your emails ASAP, communicates what you need to improve on and is overall just a great guy.

CS student here. Trevor is the most accommodating prof I've met (and will ever meet probably). The material requires practice/memorization, but there's more than enough info to study from: lectures, tutorials, weekly quizzes, assignments, and additional Qs. No exam. All resources provided online. Highly recommend as an interesting elective for CS.

I love Trevor. Im not a ling major, but he made this course fun and interesting. he was the most attentive and understanding prof I have ever had. tests were based off his lecture slides and contained only the information we needed to know, which made studying and understanding the concepts SO much easier. I recommend taking a course with him 100%

Really awesome prof! Really love the honest advice he gives for students and makes sure that his students understand the material. Always takes care of his students by making sure to not give too much work. Also love all the extra practice he gives us. Would totally recommend to take any Ling courses with him if possible!

Trevor will forever be my favorite professor. He is very clear with what he expects, and he is very reasonable with the content of the tests and the assignments, as he makes sure to relate everything to what is discussed in lecture. His office hours are very helpful, and he is super approachable! Take at least one course with him in your degree!

Would 100% take another course taught by Trevor. He genuinely cares about how students are doing and adjusts his exams accordingly. What he expects/will test is very clearly communicated and he also understands the abysmal cost of textbooks so uses none in his teaching but instead provides easy to understand lecture slides.

Trevor is fantastic in education. He knows how to make stuff easier to understand. He genuinely wants you to do well and makes things fair for students. He gives you a strong foundation in the logic needed for linguistics. I didn't know Syntax and I still found those bits of the course easy to understand because he explained clearly.

I took Ling100 as an elective this summer and Trevor was so accommodating. I was struggling in another class of mine and after explaining that to Trevor he was more than happy to give me extensions for assignments so that I could work around my other classes exams. He was so accessible via zoom& email. Best prof at SFU hands down. he's a real gem!!

Trevor is by far is the best prof I have had at SFU. He really cares about his students and wants them to do well! For the quizzes and tests the answers were all in the lectures. He is super approachable and responds fast to emails. Weekly quizzes, 3 tests, and 3 homework assignments. I would 10/10 take another course taught by him.

Trevor is an incredibly caring professor who wants nothing but success for his students. Lectures are informative and easy to follow with interesting material. He is happy to receive input from his students and makes alterations to the course to best suit the needs of students. I haven't met a better professor than Trevor.

Looking for an easy, fun, and genuinely enjoyable class? This is definitely it. Prof Block deserves all the 5/5 ratings. Really understanding and down-to-earth guy. Always open to feedback for the course and takes his job seriously with a bit of fun. Gets along with all the students and I'm sure the staff loves him as well. BEST PROF EVER!

By far the best instructor I've had so far. Incredibly clear explanations, very fair tests and assignments, super accessible outside class. I wish I could give a 100/5 on rate my prof that's how legendary Trevor is. Ling 100 covers a variety of topics in linguistics and not all of them are interesting, but Trevor makes it all so fun and cool.

I LOVE TREVOR. HE IS THE MOST UNDERSTANDING AND ACCOMMODATING PERSON I'VE EVER MET. He explains the material very well and connects with everyone on a personal and professional level. While the material is incredibly tough for a linguistics course, Trevor makes it doable and I don't dread attending his lectures!
The course is intimidating at first but its not as bad as it seems. Its easy to do well if you go to class, do the homework and ask Trevor questions. He is really sweet and very patient.

Trevor is one of the best TAs I have encountered at SFU when I was taking LING220. He is very knowledgable about the subject and knows how to explain hard theories. While taking LING200, he was an instructor and provided problems to solve in each class which is great for practice and ask questions if you get stuck. All in all, you get what you put

This class is not what I expected AT ALL. There is A LOT OF math even though this is a linguistics course but Trevor is very clear and makes it seem easy. The first exam average was 89% and it was all math!!!!!!! He is very patient and will write a big email if I have a question. I really like this class.

Do all of the homework and read all of the slides and you will do well. Trevors test questions are just like the homework and sample tests but a little harder. Funny guy too and he cares a lot about us individually.
Trevor feels like a friend helping you out with studying for a course. He is supportive and tries hard to explain things more easily for us. I hope he teaches more.

This class was hard but professor Block made lots of time for me when I need help. The later exams were hard like the final exam high score is 51/60 but then he made it out of 50. I think he is very friendly compared to most professors. I went to office hours before assignments were due and he checked my work so I have 100% on it. great guy :)

I am three weeks into this course my second time. I took a few linguistics courses already and Trevor is already the best professor yet. Well see how the first midterm goes but pick him over any other person teaching this course. I hope he teaches 300 courses because I don't want to suffer anymore lol.

I took this course with Dr Mellow and Trevor was my ta. I learned the whole course from tutorials because he could tell us the content properly. He is very nice and will spend lots of time with you if you need help. Its nice to be treated like a human at sfu and feel like someone genuinely cares about us.

the course was a lot harder than i thought it would be. trevor helped out a lot outside of class and he tried to keep the lectures funny sometimes because the stuff we learn can be boring. thats the big thing. i found out i dont like grammar. if you dont like grammar then avoid!!! the exams were really hard too but he scaled them thank god lol

Tough course but Trevor is the kindest prof I've had and is always willing to help, most likely due to the fact that he is still quite young and knows exactly what university students go through. Make sure to do the homework questions and ALL of the recommended questions as it will help you get a good sense of what is expected on tests.

This is a heavy course with lots of material, but Trevor does a great job at explaining everything by providing many different examples throughout his lecture slides, and assigning homework/assignments that are relevant to his exams. Overall, the course was quite overwhelming but I highly recommend taking a course with Trevor!

This course is really tough and the exams are really tough but Trevor knows that and gives many free marks after each exam. His examples are funny and there are many memes. There are lots of practice problems in lecture and in the homeworks but its still tough. But he will spend lots of time in office hours with you and give tips!

Trevor was teaching assistent for Dr. Heift but he is the best teaching assistent I ever had. He always explain everything very good and came up with many example until I understood it. He is very smart but he never act better than us. He said we all could be smarter than him with enough practice. He should teach lots of courses!!
